title: JStack Engineering Mastery
purpose: Train an absolute beginner to operate, maintain, audit, and evolve JStack with enterprise evidence standards.

advancementPolicy.stages0To3: Two consecutive independent attempts scoring at least 80.
advancementPolicy.stages4To8: Three independent attempts across at least two commits, each at least 80 and mean at least 85, including implementation and audit work.
advancementPolicy.stage9: Two blind capstones scoring at least 90 with correct go/no-go decisions, all seeded P0 findings, and at least 80 percent of seeded P1 findings.

stages.name: Safe Operator
stages.outcome: Orient in a repository and development environment without changing implementation/configuration or exposing secrets.
corePrinciples: Know the current path and boundary
Keep secrets out of prompts, logs, and commits
benchmarks: Identify root, branch, dirty state, runtime, tests, protected paths, and production boundary
Write only the required artifacts under .jstack-training
hardGates: No implementation, configuration, git-state, or production mutation

stages.name: JStack Reader
stages.outcome: Trace source truth through slash commands, skills, MCP schemas, handlers, policy, and packaged copies.
corePrinciples: Treat generated copies as drift risks

drills.name: Trace one command from invocation to MCP response

benchmarks: Every hop has exact file evidence
Detect at least one seeded inconsistency
hardGates: Current behavior distinguished from intended behavior

stages.name: Scoped Maintainer
stages.outcome: Deliver small coherent changes with focused tests and no unrelated churn.

drills.name: Repair a command or documentation inconsistency
Implement an isolated Python defect fix

stages.name: Debugger and Test Author
stages.outcome: Reproduce, isolate, fix, and preserve regression evidence for unseen defects.
corePrinciples: Change one variable at a time

drills.name: Diagnose an unseen parser or transport defect

benchmarks: Regression fails before and passes after

stages.name: MCP and Policy Engineer
stages.outcome: Build protocol-conformant tools and fail-closed policy enforcement with compatibility evidence.

drills.name: Add or audit one read-only MCP tool
Adversarially test malformed input and policy weakening

hardGates: No policy floor can be disabled

stages.name: Workflow Product Engineer
stages.outcome: Design consistent task-first instruction across single-lead, specialist, and full-team modes.

drills.name: Run one task through all three command modes
Validate off, embedded, coach, and assessment instruction

benchmarks: Embedded mastery capsule is at most three lines
hardGates: No eleven-role lecture in full-team output

stages.name: Packaging and Release Engineer
stages.outcome: Produce reproducible cross-platform installs, upgrades, rollback, and package parity.

drills.name: Clean install, upgrade, and rollback in temporary homes

benchmarks: macOS, Linux, and Windows CI pass
No BOM, hardcoded user path, or copy drift

stages.name: Security and Reliability Auditor
stages.outcome: Find and block unsafe execution, path, secret, dispatch, and release behavior.

drills.name: Attack policy, paths, dispatch, QA, and receipts

stages.name: JStack Architect
stages.outcome: Evolve transport, policy, evidence, or mastery state with explicit invariants and migration safety.

drills.name: Design and rehearse one control-plane evolution
Defend the architecture under independent review

stages.name: Staff Maintainer and Auditor
stages.outcome: Lead ambiguous JStack work through diagnosis, sequencing, implementation, go/no-go, release, and handoff while improving others' judgment.

drills.name: Blind audit of an unseen JStack commit
Prepare and defend an unseen release candidate

benchmarks: Catch all P0 and at least 80 percent of P1 defects
